title: atriplex patula subsp. typica
intro: Atriplex patula subsp. typica, also known as the leafy orache, is a plant species within the amaranth family. It has been used traditionally for its medicinal properties, including being a source of antioxidants and having anti-inflammatory compounds. The leafy orache is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing oxidative stress and improving digestion, as well as being used to treat various skin conditions. Atriplex patula subsp. typica is typically prepared in herbal teas or infusions, and it can also be found in dried forms for further processing into capsules, tinctures, or other supplements.
